questions.question: Explain the address-of operator (&) and dereference operator (*) in C with examples.
keyPoints: & is the address-of operator that returns the memory address of a variable
* is the dereference/indirection operator that accesses the value at a stored address
Example: int x = 5; int *p = &x; printf("%d", *p); prints 5
&x gives address, *p gives value at that address

questions.question: What is pointer arithmetic? List the arithmetic operations that can be performed on pointers.
keyPoints: Pointer arithmetic allows adding/subtracting integers to/from pointers
Operations allowed: p+n, p-n, p++, p--, ++p, --p
Pointer subtraction: p1 - p2 (gives number of elements between them
Pointer addition and comparison are NOT allowed

questions.question: Differentiate between NULL pointer and void pointer with suitable examples.
keyPoints: NULL pointer: pointer initialized to 0, does not point to valid address (int *p = NULL;)
Void pointer: generic pointer type that can point to any type (void *ptr;)
NULL can be dereferenced (undefined behavior), void cannot be dereferenced directly
Void pointers need type casting before dereferencing

questions.question: What is a pointer to a pointer? Explain with an example.
keyPoints: A pointer to a pointer stores address of another pointer
Declared with two asterisks: int **ptr
Used for dynamic 2D arrays, modifying pointers in functions
Example: int x=10, *p=&x, **q=&p; **q gives value 10

questions.question: Explain the arrow operator (->) in C and give an example where it is used.
keyPoints: -> accesses structure/union members through a pointer
Alternative to (*ptr).member notation
Example with struct: struct Student {int roll;}; struct Student s={101}, *p=&s;
p->roll is equivalent to (*p).roll and prints 101
arr decays to pointer to first element (arr[0])
Output: 30 40

questions.question: Explain all the pointer operators available in C with examples and demonstrate their use with a complete C program.
keyPoints: & (address-of): obtains memory address of variable
* (dereference): accesses value at address
+ and - (pointer arithmetic): moves pointer by specified elements
++ and --: increments/decrements pointer
Comparison operators: ==, !=, <, > for comparing addresses
->: member access through pointer
Complete program demonstrating all operators

questions.question: Write a C program to demonstrate pointer arithmetic. Show how adding an integer to a pointer and subtracting two pointers work with an integer array.
keyPoints: Initialize array and pointer
Demonstrate p+1, p+2 (shows byte offset based on type)
Show * (p+n) accessing array elements
Show pointer subtraction p2-p1 giving number of elements
Include output for each operation
Explain sizeof role in pointer arithmetic

questions.question: Explain the relationship between arrays and pointers in C. How is array name different from a pointer? Provide examples.
keyPoints: Array name decays to pointer to first element in most contexts
arr == &arr[0]
Array name is constant (cannot be reassigned), pointer is a variable
sizeof(arr) gives total array size, sizeof(ptr) gives pointer size
Pointer arithmetic works differently for arrays vs pointers
Examples showing similarities and differences
x stores value 5
p stores address of x
pp stores address of p
*p dereferences p to get x value = 5
**pp dereferences pp then p = x value = 5
All three print 5 for first printf
Second printf shows addresses: &x = address, p = same address, *pp = same address
